summary: Deterministic proof that claim discovery emits grouping signals and that Cautilus Agent curation flow audits require false-positive, false-negative, bucket, and stop-boundary review before continuing into review or eval work.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: claim discovery review clustering and curation queue
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the contract claims about deterministic grouping hints and Cautilus Agent curation-priority behavior are marked satisfied.
notClaimed: Every raw candidate is semantically correct.
LLM reviewers have already merged or removed all false positives.
Every false negative in the repo has been found.
The review queue should launch eval or HITL automatically.
Subagent review results are proven by this deterministic queue proof.

protectedBehaviors: review input groups candidates into bounded clusters and records skipped clusters
clusters preserve current labels such as review status and semantic group
action-bucket filtering focuses review on selected next-work groups
audience and semantic group separation is preserved for review clusters
deterministic discovery avoids example/probe false positives and uses candidate heuristics together
curation-flow audit passes only when discovery is followed by extraction, dedupe, false-positive, false-negative, and bucket curation language
curation-flow audit fails when the flow only runs first scan without curation language
curation-flow audit fails when the agent overruns into eval or review work
review input summary renders a readable non-table review queue
review summary CLI refuses to guess latest packets by filesystem time
claim discovery review Markdown is a projection over claim packets

decision.reason: Focused runtime tests prove review input clusters preserve source-backed labels, grouping, action buckets, and skipped work, while curation-flow audit tests require false-positive, false-negative, dedupe, bucket, and stop-boundary handling before the agent continues into review or eval work.
